ksa_text: Knowledge of data administration policies and procedures, including data lifecycle management, access control and security, data retention and archival, compliance documentation, and stewardship responsibilities.

dcwf.top_element_label: * Knowledge of risk management processes (e.g., methods for assessing and mitigating risk).

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: * Knowledge of risk management processes (e.g., methods for assessing and mitigating risk).

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Edge-domain Data Quality KSA with insufficient signal strength (composite score 0.411) to justify inclusion for the Director of Analytics. Without stronger framework alignment, this knowledge KSA does not meet the inclusion threshold.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No internal inconsistency identified. Domain scope, seniority fit, and signal level are internally consistent with exclusion.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Could this edge-domain knowledge KSA be expected in real-world Director of Analytics roles, even if not core to the role definition?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Job market analysis and framework role definitions confirm Data Quality is not a standard competency expectation for Director of Analytics. Edge-case exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence does not support including this edge-domain KSA in the Director of Analytics competency profile. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Insufficient signal (score 0.411) and edge-domain placement (Data Quality) do not meet inclusion threshold for the Director of Analytics. All passes support exclusion.
